messages.subject: [R-jobs] Job: R Package Developer / Consulting Statistician
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Job: R Package Developer / Consulting Statistician
messages.body_plain:
R Package Developer / Consulting Statistician 
Burlington, Vermont

Precision Bioassay is a small and growing statistical consulting
firm specializing in the design and analysis of biological assays of
pharmaceutical proteins and vaccines. We are building a turnkey software
solution for cell culture bioassay that integrates optimal design of
bioassay experiments, implementation of designs using robotic pipettors,
and potency analysis using linear and nonlinear mixed models. This
software is intended for use in a highly regulated industry. Portions
of the code development for this project will be assigned to you.

Role/Responsibilities

  * Function as an integral member of a team of highly-skilled professionals
  * Elicit and document functional requirements and specifications
  * Collaborate in development of core analytic modules (coded in R)
  * Participate in development of web-based GUI to core analytic modules
  * Analyze bioassay data for clients in assay development and validation, 
    sometimes requiring development of novel methodologies

Qualifications

  * Three or more years experience in developing packages in the R language 
  * Experience in either building web interfaces for technical applications 
    or building database-integrated applications in R 
  * Experience with applications and theory of linear and nonlinear 
    mixed effects models
  * Good writing and communication skills 
  * MS or PhD in Statistics, Biostatistics, or equivalent 

Additional Desired Experience

  * Fluency in administrating Linux for a multi-platform environment 
  * Experience configuring and administering Apache 
  * Experience with JavaScript, PHP, Ruby, Python, and/or MySQL 
  * Experience with interfaces to equipment, especially lab automation 
  * Statistical consulting experience in biotechnology, nonclinical 
    pharmaceutical or agricultural applications 

Reports to: Dr. David Lansky

Classification: Permanent, full-time position.

Benefits: Comprehensive package including health and dental insurance,
  short and long term disability, life insurance, 401k, 
  generous time off policy, and flex time.

Pay: Commensurate with experience.

To Apply: Send cover letter and resume by US mail or email to: 

  Precision Bioassay
  257 S Union St 
  Burlington, Vermont 05401 USA
  802/865-0155
  info at precisionbioassay.com (ATTN: Sarah Guth)

Precision Bioassay is an equal opportunity employer.  Women and people of 
color are encouraged to apply.

messages.subject: [R-jobs] Quick programming in R calling social network routines?
messages.subject_clean: [R-jobs] Quick programming in R calling social network routines?
messages.body_plain:
I'm looking for someone to do a quick freelance programming in R to do some
network analysis. I'm a researcher with no knowledge in R (doing all my
statistics analysis in Stata and network analysis in  UCINET). I would like
to quickly test the possibility to do batch network analyses using R.

 

The freelance should therefore write a small program that would take as
input a file describing a bunch of social network (provided), then call some
routines that exist in the social network package provided at
http://erzuli.ss.uci.edu/R.stuff/. It would then produce both some measures
per network, and some measures per individual in each network.

 

I believe this thing may take only a few dozen lines for someone who has a
reasonable command of R; overall, no less than a few hours for someone with
good command of the language, mainly just to understand the logic of the
social network of the routines (I can explain fully). The reason I want to
outsource it is that it would take me days (or worse) to get it to work.

 

Once I get it, I'll decide whether and how I move forward.

 

Please, do let me know (fcaspam at yahoo.fr) if you're interested.

 

Fab 


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]
